body:
DIRECTIVE REFINEMENT — supersedes Addition 3 (win-rate handling) of the prior addendum (09ce7e02). Everything else in that addendum (reverse-mining engine, more samples) stands. This corrects and sharpens the win-rate rule per Manuel, and it is BACKED BY A NEW TEST.

== THE CORRECTED UNDERSTANDING: WIN-RATE AND EDGE COEXIST ==
Manuel is right: win-rate and edge are NOT inherently opposed, and win-rate is a legitimate measure of how well the system performs. The earlier framing was too blunt. The precise truth, now TESTED:
 - The real tradeoff is NOT 'win-rate vs edge'. It is 'win-rate vs PAYOFF GEOMETRY, at a FIXED read'. Sliding TP closer buys hit-rate by shrinking each win -- that seesaw is the ONLY thing to forbid.
 - When the READ improves (better perception of the moment), win-rate AND edge rise TOGETHER at constant geometry. VERIFIED on 65k bars at fixed TP2.0/SL1.2: as conditioning sharpened -- no read -> 1 -> 2 -> 3 conditions -- win-rate rose 38.0% -> 39.6% -> 40.5% -> 40.9% AND edge rose +0.010 -> +0.049 -> +0.073 -> +0.080. Both climbed together, geometry never touched.
 - Therefore win-rate is a FIRST-CLASS MEASURE OF READ-QUALITY. A rising win-rate at fixed geometry is direct evidence the system reads the market better. This is exactly what to maximise.

== THE RULE (replaces the prior win-rate rule) ==
 1. Win-rate is a first-class reported measure of read-quality, tracked on every edge and on the honesty dashboard alongside expectancy. Raising it is a genuine goal, not merely a comfort metric.
 2. MAXIMISE win-rate the RIGHT way: by sharpening the READ -- better conditioning, better lens-intersection (the nine lenses), better reverse-mined signatures, better relationship/interaction detection. Any win-rate gain achieved at CONSTANT payoff geometry is a real improvement in perception; pursue it hard. Both win-rate and edge should climb together as the system gets smarter, and that joint climb is the primary success signal.
 3. The ONLY forbidden move: manufacturing win-rate by pulling the target closer / widening the stop (changing geometry to buy hit-rate at the cost of expectancy). That is the seesaw, and it silently trades money away. Geometry is chosen ONLY to maximise replicated net-of-cost expectancy (the L2 exit search), never to hit a win-rate number.
 4. Both can and should coexist: strive for high win-rate AND high edge, achieved together through superior reading. When a genuine high-win-rate edge exists that also holds expectancy out-of-sample, it is the best kind of edge -- prefer it, press it, size it up. There is NO ceiling imposed on win-rate; there is only a ban on faking it via geometry.
 5. Report, per edge: win-rate, expectancy, and payoff geometry together, so it is always visible whether a win-rate change came from a better READ (both rise -- good) or from moved GEOMETRY (seesaw -- flagged).

== SUCCESS SIGNAL ==
The clearest sign TUNGSTEN is genuinely improving: win-rate AND expectancy rising together across passes at stable geometry. Track this joint trend on the honesty dashboard and in the competence vector. A pass that raised win-rate while holding or improving expectancy at constant geometry is a pass that made the system genuinely SMARTER -- report it as such. A pass that raised win-rate while dropping expectancy moved the geometry seesaw and is flagged, not celebrated.

STANDING LAW unchanged: expectancy net-of-cost is the objective; win-rate is maximised through better reading, never through geometry; replicate-or-discard; power-gate; survival before growth; Zero-Regression Law; live only on Manuel's explicit go-ahead. The aim, corrected and sharpened: a system whose sharpening PERCEPTION lifts win-rate and edge together -- both high, both real, both earned by reading the market better, never by cashing out early.
